metadata.title: Build a Constraint Extractor with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I want to build a constraint extractor because my briefs bury their real requirements in loose prose. I understand constraints are the limits that make an output pass or fail, distinct from the task itself. Help me: (1) design a prompt that reads my messy project brief and pulls out every hard constraint, hidden or explicit; (2) have it separate genuine constraints from mere preferences; (3) show me how to feed the extracted list back into a generation prompt so nothing important silently drops.

props.command: I want to build a constraint extractor because my briefs bury their real requirements in loose prose. I understand constraints are the limits that make an output pass or fail, distinct from the task itself. Help me: (1) design a prompt that reads my messy project brief and pulls out every hard constraint, hidden or explicit; (2) have it separate genuine constraints from mere preferences; (3) show me how to feed the extracted list back into a generation prompt so nothing important silently drops.
props.segment: Build a Constraint Extractor with Claude

beats.label: Hook — five types
beats.narration: The five types of constraints in a prompt serve completely different functions. Conflating them is why prompts fail in ways that are hard to diagnose — because the failure looks different depending on which type is missing.

shots.notes: Five labeled tiles in a row: SCOPE | EVIDENCE | UNCERTAINTY | PRIVACY | TONE. Each with a one-line failure mode below it in CRIMSON. Layout: horizontal band across center of frame. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Claude command
beats.narration: The seed command pipes a prompt into Claude and asks it to classify every constraint into one of five types, name the failure mode if that constraint were removed, and flag any type with zero entries.

props.command: claude "Parse this prompt and classify every constraint into one of five types: scope, evidence, uncertainty, privacy, tone. For each: type, failure mode if removed. Flag any type with zero entries." < prompt.txt

beats.label: Python code
beats.narration: The script reads the prompt, extracts the constraint inventory from Claude, and generates a constraint map showing which types are populated and which are missing.

props.code:
import subprocess, sys
prompt = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
extract_cmd = (
  "Classify every constraint into: scope, evidence, "
  "uncertainty, privacy, tone. For each: type + "
  "failure mode if removed. Flag empty types."
)
result = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", extract_cmd],
  input=prompt, capture_output=True, text=True
)
print(result.stdout)

beats.label: Constraint inventory
beats.narration: The inventory shows scope and evidence constraints populated, tone partially filled, and uncertainty and privacy empty. Each empty slot shows the specific failure mode its absence enables.

shots.notes: Five-row constraint inventory table. Columns: TYPE | COUNT | FAILURE MODE IF MISSING. SCOPE: 2 (green). EVIDENCE: 1 (gold). UNCERTAINTY: 0 (crimson) — 'will state speculative claims as fact'. PRIVACY: 0 (crimson) — 'may include identifiers in output'. TONE: 1 (gold). CRIMSON rows glow for empty types.

beats.label: The change — add missing type
beats.narration: Add one uncertainty constraint to the prompt — 'if you are uncertain, say so rather than speculating' — and re-run. Watch the uncertainty row fill and its failure mode disappear.

props.command: claude "Classify constraints again after adding: 'If uncertain, state it rather than speculating.'" < prompt_updated.txt

beats.label: Changed output
beats.narration: Uncertainty type: 0 to 1. Failure mode row: gone. The constraint map completes and the specific failure disappears.

shots.notes: BEFORE: UNCERTAINTY row — 0 constraints, CRIMSON glowing failure mode. AFTER: UNCERTAINTY row — 1 constraint, green, failure mode removed. Arrow between BEFORE and AFTER states. Caption: 'One constraint closes one failure mode.'

beats.label: Teardown angle
beats.narration: Constraint types are not interchangeable. A prompt with strong scope and weak uncertainty handling will confidently state things it doesn't know. Knowing which type is missing tells you exactly what failure to expect.

shots.notes: Large text: 'Know the type. Know the failure.' Below: 'Strong scope + weak uncertainty = confident hallucination.' CRIMSON on the failure consequence. Teardown palette, CRIMSON rule.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Build a Constraint Extractor with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
